data.title: Tempus AI Gilead Expansion Deepens Role In Oncology Drug Development
data.description:
• Tempus AI (NasdaqGS:TEM) expanded its multi year collaboration with Gilead Sciences, giving Gilead broader enterprise access to Tempus’ Lens platform and multimodal data.
  • The agreement extends the use of Tempus’ AI tools across Gilead’s oncology research and drug development programs, covering multiple cancer indications.
  • The expansion focuses on clinical trial design, biomarker work, and real world evidence generation for oncology pipelines.

For you as an investor, this development reinforces where Tempus AI sits in the healthcare AI space, supplying AI driven analytics and multimodal data to large biopharma partners. The company’s platforms are being tied directly to oncology workflows, from early research through clinical development, in an area where large drug makers continue to invest in data and diagnostics.

The broader use of Lens across Gilead’s oncology programs could influence how Tempus AI positions its products with other partners and within the healthcare AI ecosystem. Investors watching NasdaqGS:TEM may treat this as a reference point for understanding the company’s role in AI enabled drug development and how similar collaborations might shape its future business mix.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Tempus AI by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Tempus AI.

2 things going right for Tempus AI that this headline doesn't cover.

This expanded Gilead agreement takes Tempus AI further into the core of oncology decision making at a large pharmaceutical group, moving from project by project work to broader, enterprise level use of its Lens platform. For you, that points to Tempus AI being treated less as a point solution vendor and more as a long term data and analytics partner, similar to how companies such as IQVIA or Flatiron Health have been positioned for drug makers like Roche or Bristol Myers Squibb. The dedicated Tempus analytical services built into the deal also matter, because they tie human expertise to the software and data, which can deepen integration into trial design, biomarker work and real world evidence studies across multiple cancer indications.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Tempus AI Narrative

  • The broader Lens rollout at Gilead aligns with the narrative focus on biopharma collaborations and the use of Tempus AI’s multimodal data to support oncology research, reinforcing the idea that large pharma is embedding its tools into R&D workflows.
  • At the same time, greater reliance on large clients such as Gilead highlights one of the narrative risks, where changes in pharma R&D budgets or data priorities could affect the visibility of Tempus AI’s data and analytics revenue.
  • The explicit integration of dedicated analytical services and enterprise wide access may not be fully reflected in earlier narrative assumptions that focused more on diagnostics and individual partnerships rather than deeper platform level usage.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Tempus AI to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Concentration risk if a meaningful share of Tempus AI’s data and analytics revenue depends on a small group of pharma partners, leaving results sensitive to contract decisions.
  • ⚠️ Execution risk in scaling enterprise wide deployments across multiple cancer indications, where delays in integration or lower than expected usage could limit the impact of the collaboration.
  • 🎁 Expanded access to Lens at Gilead may support higher data utilization across oncology programs, strengthening Tempus AI’s position as an AI powered research partner to large drug makers.
  • 🎁 The agreement may serve as a reference point when Tempus AI speaks with other pharma groups that also work with data focused peers such as IQVIA, Flatiron Health or ConcertAI, potentially supporting future partnership discussions.

What To Watch Going Forward

Following this announcement, focus on how often Tempus AI references concrete use cases from the Gilead collaboration, such as specific trial design wins, biomarker programs or real world evidence outputs. Pay attention to any disclosures about the mix of revenue from data and analytics partnerships versus diagnostics, and to analyst commentary on client concentration or contract duration. The share price move of 7.03% after the news shows that the market is still debating how to price these relationships, so future updates that clarify adoption levels, renewal activity and any expansion to additional indications will be important for assessing how durable this pharma facing part of the business could be.

data.title: Is It Time To Reassess Ringkjøbing Landbobank (CPSE:RILBA) After Strong Five Year Gains
data.description:
• Wondering whether Ringkjøbing Landbobank is fairly priced or starting to look interesting at current levels? This article walks through what the numbers are saying about the stock’s value.
  • The shares last closed at DKK 1,574, with returns of 0.6% over 7 days, a 1.4% decline over 30 days, 1.9% year to date, 44.6% over 1 year, 62.5% over 3 years and 163.6% over 5 years. These figures may influence how you think about upside and risk today.

Approach 1: Ringkjøbing Landbobank Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much value a bank creates above the return that equity investors typically require, then capitalises those “excess” profits into an estimated intrinsic value per share.

For Ringkjøbing Landbobank, the starting point is a Book Value of DKK475.19 per share and an Average Return on Equity of 21.72%. Based on the median return on equity from the past 5 years, the model uses a Stable EPS of DKK121.09 per share. The implied Cost of Equity is DKK34.38 per share, which leaves an Excess Return of DKK86.71 per share, suggesting earnings above the required return on shareholders’ capital.

The model also factors in a Stable Book Value of DKK557.60 per share, using weighted future book value estimates from 2 analysts. When these excess returns are projected and discounted, the Excess Returns model arrives at an intrinsic value of about DKK2,571.92 per share. Compared with the recent share price of DKK1,574, this implies the stock trades at a 38.8% discount, which the model labels as undervalued.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Approach 2: Ringkjøbing Landbobank Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much you are paying for each unit of earnings. It ties the share price directly to the profits that belong to shareholders, which helps you compare different banks on a like for like basis.

What counts as a "normal" or "fair" P/E often reflects how the market views a company’s growth prospects and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher multiple, while slower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower one.

Ringkjøbing Landbobank currently trades on a P/E of 16.36x. That sits above the Danish Banks industry average of 11.16x and the peer average of 11.60x. Simply Wall St’s proprietary Fair Ratio for the stock is 12.77x, which represents the P/E that might be expected given factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk profile.

The Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the industry because it adjusts for company specific characteristics rather than assuming all banks deserve the same multiple. Compared with the current P/E of 16.36x, the Fair Ratio of 12.77x suggests the shares trade at a premium.

Result: OVERVALUED

From here, focus on three things. First, the independent expert’s conclusion in the booklet and whether it views the scheme as fair and reasonable. Second, any updates from Brookfield, GIC or National Storage REIT on conditions such as regulatory or court approvals. Approach 1: General Motors Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a company is worth today by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to a present value.

For General Motors, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month free cash flow stands at about $16.29b, and analysts provide explicit forecasts out to 2027, with Simply Wall St extrapolating further to 2035. For example, projected free cash flow for 2026 is $10.45b and for 2035 it is $12.76b, all in $ terms.

Discounting these cash flows back to today produces an estimated intrinsic value of $120.35 per share. Compared with the current share price, this DCF model indicates the stock is 36.2% undervalued.

For readers, the key takeaway is that under these cash flow assumptions the current price is lower than the modelled value of the underlying business.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests General Motors is undervalued by 36.2%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 62 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for General Motors.

Approach 2: General Motors Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like General Motors, the P/E ratio is often a useful quick check because it tells you how many dollars you are paying for each dollar of current earnings.

What counts as a “normal” P/E usually reflects how the market views a company’s growth potential and risk. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk tends to come with a lower multiple.

General Motors currently trades on a P/E of 21.8x. That sits above the Auto industry average of 17.1x, but below the peer group average of 56.8x, which suggests investors are pricing it more cautiously than some peers but higher than the broader industry.

Simply Wall St’s “Fair Ratio” is a proprietary P/E estimate that blends factors such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and company specific risks into a single number. This gives a more tailored anchor for valuation than a simple comparison with industry averages or peers, which may have very different profiles.

For General Motors, the Fair Ratio is 25.7x, compared with the current P/E of 21.8x, which indicates that the shares are trading below that modelled fair level.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your General Motors Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives are simply your way of attaching a clear story about General Motors to concrete numbers like fair value, future revenue, earnings and margins, so that your view of the company connects directly to a financial forecast and then to a fair value that can be compared with today’s price.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an accessible tool used by millions of investors. They allow you to pick or create a storyline, such as a cautious view that ties General Motors to a fair value of about US$41.79 or a more optimistic view that ties it to about US$122.00, and then see how each story translates into different assumptions about growth, profitability and risk.

Once a Narrative is chosen, the platform continuously refreshes it when new information like news or earnings is added. This means you can quickly see whether your chosen fair value still makes sense next to the live share price and use that comparison to decide whether the current price feels attractive, fully valued, or expensive relative to the story you believe.

For General Motors, however, we will make it really easy for you with previews of two leading General Motors Narratives:

The first reflects a more optimistic stance that ties today's share price to higher long term earnings power, while the second leans cautious and assumes the current price is ahead of what the business fundamentals justify.

Use them as two clear reference points and decide which set of assumptions feels closer to your own view of how the company might progress over the next few years.

🐂 General Motors Bull Case

  • Assumes GM's investments in EVs, U.S. manufacturing, and battery capacity support earnings and margins, even with tariff and regulatory headwinds.

🐻 General Motors Bear Case

  • Assumes GM faces a tough backdrop for manufacturing and operations, which affects how confidently it can commit to its product lineup.
